Hey guys, JB in the house. Time for Throwback Thursday. Got an awesome clip for you here. Alright, check this out right now. It's on Scripting of course. Just check it out and I'll see you on the other side. The idea of scripts turns people off because they don't understand what a script really is. Number one, to get that a script is a work in progress. That's not what I'm talking about here. A script, what a script represents. It's your best effort. Your most coherent case on both a logical and an emotional level said in the perfect words and the perfect tonality. It's perfect, perfect, perfect, perfect. It's your ability. If you have a script, just imagine if you could go back to the best 50 sales you ever made and write down all those days you sounded so great and so congruent and everyone was just laying down. That's what a script is. It's your best effort. You have to use scripts when you sell. I don't mean reading from a script. That's the last thing you want to do. I'm working from a script right now. You know where the script is? Anyone want to guess? Anybody? My script is in my head. I'm not winging this as I go along. I memorized it all. I know it all. By the back of my hand, I worked for thousands of hours to put this stuff together because I know that if I'm going to truly operate the highest level, they pay me. I'm not going to come out and wing it. When you go into a sales situation, if you go into wing it, you're off your rocker. It's insane. If I was in your business, I would know exactly what I was going to say before I said it. Not just in the moment, but for the whole entire presentation. I'd have three language patterns for my opening, making my case. I'd have three airtight logic cases for my product, three for myself, three for my company that were elegant. They were the best of the best of the best. Now you're saying, well, you know, I don't want to sound like I'm reading from a script. Let me tell you something. Who's gone to the movies here? Raise your hand. Everyone's gone to the movies, right? You think they're reading from scripts in the movies? You ever see a movie with a really shitty script? It's unwatchable. Everything in the movies that sounds brave for all your favorite movies, everything that you emotionally connect with them, watched over and over again. It was being read from a script, but actors are trained to sound like they're not reading from scripts. That's what you have to do as a salesperson. All right, pretty awesome, right? Listen, just remember here, you know, the script is not just about the airtight logical case. We use all words, script, script equal words, right, to paint a picture of how the prospect will feel in the future using a proc and feeling good. You understand? So we use the script to future pace, which moves someone emotionally. That's why scripts are so crucially create both logical and emotional. So, dear, right? Love you guys.
